title: NGE From Misatos point of view
display_title: NGE From Misatos point of view
wikitext:
[]: NGE: From Misato's point of view?             Quote Post by Stuart » Mon Nov 01, 2010 5:25 am I've always thought of NGE as being presented through two principle viewpoints: Shinji's and Misato's. Shinji is suddenly thrown from his mundane life into the mad world of Eva, knowing nothing about it (as do we). However, Shinji's interest in the Evas and Angels is nothing but passive curiosity (a few comments here and there throughout the series). However, the first character we meet is Misato, she speaks the opening line of the series, and she is the one who shares the viewer's interest in getting to the bottom of the mysteries. She's being misled by Gendo and Ritsuko (as Anno misleads us), and things are revealed to her (and the viewer) which aren't revealed to Shinji. Of course, there's no absolute "point of view" in Evangelion, as we see snippets of Seele's meetings etc (although the dialogue and any of these scene doesn't give much away to the viewer). I've always thought that Misato is the strongest character; she doesn't let Kaji's death destroy her, she's always searching for the truth, and acting in the best interests of the pilots. Shinji, on the other hand, is self-pitying, and lets the whole situation completely break him down. So the two characters are there to contrast one another too. Ritsuko accuses Misato at various points of having a vendetta against the Angels; a strange point really seeing as half of mankind have been killed by them (perhaps in Ritsuko's view it was a mankind who was responsible for his own destruction, as she implies in her pre-Dummy Destruction speech). Still, she's not acting for herself, she's acting to avenge her father (even if Ritsuko is right). It's Misato who saves the day in Air; she's the one who drags Shinji to Unit One, and risks her own life (and loses it) in the process. Misato's last lines are poignant because they show just how little she has done for herself in the course of the series - she didn't even change the carpet in her flat. She asks Kaji if she's done the right thing on her dying breath - she has no arrogant certainty about her actions, unlike Ritsuko (before she cracks up), Gendo and Seele. Rather, she's always trying to make sure what she is doing is the right thing for others. So what say the EvaGeeks? Is Misato's point of view actually the main viewpoint in Eva rather than Shinji's? Or is it there as a balance to Shinji's? Or am I completely wrong?

[]: Re: NGE: From Misato's point of view?             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Nov 01, 2010 6:50 am Stuart wrote: Is Misato's point of view actually the main viewpoint in Eva rather than Shinji's? Or is it there as a balance to Shinji's? Or am I completely wrong?

[]: Re: NGE: From Misato's point of view?             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Nov 01, 2010 12:20 pm Stuart wrote: Still, she's not acting for herself, she's acting to avenge her father (even if Ritsuko is right).

[]: Re: NGE: From Misato's point of view?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 01, 2010 1:25 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Pardon, but isn't pursuing your own vendetta acting for yourself?

[]: Re: NGE: From Misato's point of view?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 22, 2010 3:36 am Stuart wrote: Shinji's interest in the Evas and Angels is nothing but passive curiosity
